stubs_used: Heredoc init.sh attempt failed — fell back to manual project skeleton creation (per runbook fallback path). Mimicked init.sh by copying scripts, templates, platform module, CI/release pipelines, and writing .claude/{phase-state,process-state,manifest,build-progress,settings}.json.
GitHub remote — used https://example.com/fake.git via host=other (configured in manifest.json + git remote add origin); no push attempted because we bypassed the create_and_protect_remote flow.
Phase 2 security audit — created docs/security-audits/uat-feat-1-security-audit.md with Open=false; no real Semgrep run.
Phase 3 SAST — touched docs/test-results/<DATE>_semgrep_pass.json instead of running Semgrep.
Phase 3 accessibility — touched docs/test-results/<DATE>_lighthouse_accessibility.json instead of running Lighthouse.
Phase 3 performance — touched docs/test-results/<DATE>_lighthouse_performance.json instead of running Lighthouse.
Phase 3 integration — touched docs/test-results/<DATE>_integration.json instead of running real integration suite.
Python source/test — only stub `# stub` files in src/ and tests/; no Python build/test was run.
Pre-commit hook simulation — pre-commit-gate.sh was tested by feeding the JSON envelope on stdin since this UAT run is not inside a real Claude Code PreToolUse cycle.
Phase advancement — runbook expects gate to advance current_phase, but check-phase-gate.sh is a consistency check only. Manually bumped current_phase via jq for phase 2/3/4 transitions.
lockfile — touched poetry.lock to satisfy phase2_init project_scaffolded gate.
init_driver: FALLBACK PATH USED. Heredoc with canned answers (uat-agent-12, description, platform=2 mcp-server, track=3 full, confirm-full=y, deployment=1 personal, language=2 python, project_dir=blank, continue=blank, proceed=y, host=4 other, visibility=1 private, https://example.com/fake.git, attest=yes) caused init.sh to enter an infinite 'Invalid choice. Enter a number between 1 and 5.' loop after stdin EOF — prompt_choice's while-true loop has no EOF/timeout escape, so once the heredoc is exhausted the next read returns empty and the loop spins forever. Process was hung consuming a CPU and writing >11MB log of the same 'Invalid choice' line. Killed via kill -9. Fell back to manual project skeleton creation (per runbook section A guidance) by copying scripts, templates, CI/release pipelines, platform module, generating .claude/{phase-state,process-state,manifest,build-progress,settings}.json by hand. All remaining sections (B–G) ran against this hand-built skeleton.

tests.name: init_sh_exits_zero
tests.details: init.sh hung in prompt_choice's while-true 'Invalid choice' loop after stdin EOF; killed with SIGKILL. No exit code recorded. The loop wrote >11MB of identical 'Invalid choice. Enter a number between 1 and 5.' lines while consuming a full CPU.

framework_bugs.summary: init.sh hangs in CPU-burning loop on stdin EOF
framework_bugs.reproduction: Pipe a heredoc with N answers into `bash init.sh`. If N is one short of the actual prompt count (which depends on installed-tool state, Docker presence, Confirm? prompts, etc.), the next prompt_choice() call's while-true loop receives EOF on every read, prints `Invalid choice. Enter a number between 1 and ${#options[@]}.`, and immediately retries — forever. Process burns one full CPU core and writes the same line to its output stream until killed. In my run the log grew to 11.4 MB in seconds.
framework_bugs.expected: prompt_choice (and prompt_input) in scripts/lib/helpers.sh should detect stdin EOF (e.g. `read || break`) and either (a) bail with a clear error, or (b) accept defaults / prompt to retry interactively. Without an EOF guard, ANY scripted invocation that under-feeds answers will hang indefinitely.
framework_bugs.actual: scripts/lib/helpers.sh:155-172 — `while true; do read -rp ... choice; if [[valid]]; then echo; return; fi; echo Invalid choice; done`. No `read || break`, no max-retry counter, no EOF check. After stdin EOF, the read returns empty immediately and the loop spins.

framework_bugs.summary: init.sh has no scriptable mode for UAT/CI
framework_bugs.reproduction: init.sh --help lists only --dry-run and --help. No --non-interactive, --answers-from FILE, --project, --platform, --track, --scenario, --upgrade-from flags exist despite the runbook documenting them.
framework_bugs.expected: An --answers-from FILE flag (or full set of --project/--platform/etc. flags) so that UAT, CI, and `solo-orchestrator init` automation can drive deterministically without a fragile heredoc that depends on installed-tool state.
framework_bugs.actual: Only --dry-run and --help. The actual prompt count varies with whether Docker/jq/git/node are installed, whether Colima or Docker Desktop is the chosen runtime, whether Qdrant container is running, whether intake-progress.json exists, etc. No canonical answer-count calculator exists.

framework_bugs.summary: Phase 4 monitoring_configured artifact gate requires HANDOFF.md but step ordering puts handoff_written AFTER monitoring_configured
framework_bugs.reproduction: Run Phase 4 in the documented step order: production_build → rollback_tested → go_live_verified → monitoring_configured. The monitoring_configured artifact check (process-checklist.sh ~line 351) requires HANDOFF.md to exist AND contain 'monitoring|error tracking|sentry|crashlytics|uptimerobot'. But the next step (handoff_written) is the one that creates HANDOFF.md. So you cannot complete monitoring_configured until you've already done the work for handoff_written.
framework_bugs.expected: Either reorder PHASE4_STEPS to put handoff_written before monitoring_configured, or relax monitoring_configured's gate to accept evidence in another file (e.g. docs/test-results/<date>_monitoring-config.md) when HANDOFF.md isn't yet written.
framework_bugs.actual: PHASE4_STEPS=(production_build rollback_tested go_live_verified monitoring_configured handoff_written handoff_tested) at process-checklist.sh:32. The artifact gate at lines 348-360 short-circuits monitoring_configured because handoff_written hasn't run. I worked around it by manually writing HANDOFF.md with monitoring keywords before completing monitoring_configured.

framework_bugs.summary: No script advances current_phase between phases (regression of agent-1 finding)
framework_bugs.reproduction: Complete all phaseN_X process steps; run scripts/check-phase-gate.sh; .claude/phase-state.json `.current_phase` is unchanged. There is no `--advance` or `--approve-gate` subcommand on check-phase-gate.sh, check-gate.sh, or process-checklist.sh that flips current_phase.
framework_bugs.expected: A documented script (e.g., `scripts/process-checklist.sh --advance-phase` or `scripts/check-phase-gate.sh --approve`) that, after gate consistency + approval-log entry, atomically writes the new current_phase value.
framework_bugs.actual: Phase advancement is left to manual jq editing or to the human Orchestrator's discretion. The runbook *asserts* `phase_state_advances_to_2` etc. as test cases, which means the framework's phase model and the runbook's protocol are out of sync. Confirms agent-1's finding is reproducible on a different scenario/platform/track combination.

framework_bugs.summary: test-gate.sh --record-feature emits 'integer expression expected' bash warning
framework_bugs.reproduction: `bash scripts/test-gate.sh --record-feature uat-feat-1` after a fresh init produces: `scripts/test-gate.sh: line 122: [: null: integer expression expected`. The recording succeeds but the warning leaks to stderr and shows up in any captured logs.
framework_bugs.expected: Coerce the variable to an integer (e.g. `${val:-0}`) before the numeric comparison, or use jq's `// 0` default in the upstream extraction.
framework_bugs.actual: Line 122 compares a value that can be `null` (when the feature counter is unset) against an integer with `[`. Output: `[: null: integer expression expected`.

framework_bugs.summary: lint-uat-scenarios.sh reports false-positive on platform reference files
framework_bugs.reproduction: init.sh copies tests/uat/examples/scenario-reference.json (a populated JSON scenario) and tests/uat/examples/pre-flight-reference.html. Running `bash scripts/lint-uat-scenarios.sh tests/uat/examples/scenario-reference.json` (or the html) prints: `No scenarios block found — is the file populated? (expected 'const scenarios = [...]')`. The lint script only recognizes inline JS arrays in HTML, but the canonical co-build artifact is JSON. References shipped by init.sh fail their own lint.
framework_bugs.expected: Either lint should accept JSON scenario files (`*-scenario.json`), or init.sh should not ship reference files that fail lint, or lint should be scoped to `tests/uat/sessions/*.html` only.
framework_bugs.actual: Files shipped by init.sh fail lint with confusing messaging.

documentation_gaps.gap: Runbook init driver section needs explicit warning that prompt_choice has no EOF guard — a one-answer-short heredoc will hang indefinitely. Should recommend timeout(1) wrapper around init.sh or that agents kill init.sh after fixed wall-clock if no project dir appears.
Number/order of init.sh prompts is undocumented and varies with installed-tool state. Confirms agent-1's gap finding.
documentation_gaps.where: init.sh collect_project_info / resolve_and_install_tools / create_and_protect_remote — no consolidated prompt-sequence doc in docs/.

documentation_gaps.gap: PHASE2_INIT, PHASE1_STEPS, PHASE3_STEPS, PHASE4_STEPS, UAT_STEPS, BUILD_LOOP_STEPS arrays are defined only in scripts/process-checklist.sh — not exposed via `--list-steps` and not documented in the builders guide.

PHASE4 step ordering (monitoring_configured before handoff_written) is contradicted by monitoring_configured's artifact gate that requires HANDOFF.md. Either the order or the gate is wrong; the documentation says nothing about which is canonical.
documentation_gaps.where: scripts/process-checklist.sh:32 (PHASE4_STEPS) vs lines 348-360 (monitoring_configured gate) vs docs/reference/builders-guide.md (Phase 4 sequence).

documentation_gaps.gap: verify-install.sh prints `__PLATFORM__` / `__LANGUAGE__` / `__TRACK__` placeholders in its INFO header on a freshly-fallback-built project (because tool-preferences.json was missing). When init.sh's tool-preferences.json is present these substitute, but the lack of substitution is silent and misleading.

phase2_init step name `backup_restore_tested` referenced by --verify-init is not a valid step (process-checklist rejects with 'Unknown step backup_restore_tested for process phase2_init'; valid name is data_model_applied + something else). The verify-init message and the actual step list disagree.
documentation_gaps.where: scripts/process-checklist.sh — phase2_init steps list vs verify-init guidance text.

tests.name: phase1_steps_array_present
tests.details: jq '.phase1_architecture.steps_completed' returned [].

tests.name: each_phase1_step_completes
tests.details: All 5 steps (architecture_selected, threat_model_complete, data_model_defined, ui_scaffolding_done, bible_synthesized) completed in order with exit 0. Phase 1 has no per-step artifact gate (unlike build_loop:security_audit and Phase 4 steps), so stub completion succeeds.

tests.name: phase1_to_phase2_gate_passes
tests.details: scripts/check-phase-gate.sh exits 0 after all steps complete and reports 'Phase gates consistent.' HOWEVER — it is a *consistency check*, not a phase advancer. It does NOT update .phase-state.json.current_phase.

tests.name: phase_state_advances_to_2
tests.details: current_phase did NOT auto-advance after the gate. Per agent-1's finding (still unfixed), no script in scripts/ does this advancement. I bumped current_phase to 2 manually via jq to continue testing.

tests.name: start_feature_records_state
tests.details: After --start-feature uat-feat-1, .build_loop.feature = 'uat-feat-1' and .build_loop.started_at populated.

tests.name: each_step_completes_in_order
tests.details: Sequential complete-step calls (tests_written → tests_verified_failing → implemented → security_audit → documentation_updated → feature_recorded) all succeeded with stub artifacts. The build_loop:security_audit gate checks docs/security-audits/<feature-slug>-security-audit.md exists; my stub satisfied it.

tests.name: pre_commit_passes_after_loop
tests.details: git commit -m 'feat(uat-1): stub feature for UAT' succeeded after all 5 mandatory build-loop steps were complete. Note: my fallback skeleton did NOT install .git/hooks/pre-commit (init.sh's install_precommit_hook function was not exercised), so the commit was checked only by the PreToolUse simulation, not by a real pre-commit hook.

tests.name: bl006_blocks_feat_without_loop
tests.details: Two-state test: (a) Immediately after completing a 6/6 build_loop with .build_loop.feature still set, a new `feat(...)` commit was ALLOWED (no block). (b) After clearing .build_loop.feature = null, a new `feat(...)` commit was correctly DENIED via PreToolUse with the canonical 'no Build Loop active' reason. So BL-006 enforcement is intact, but the implicit assumption that 'feature_recorded' clears the loop is wrong — recording a feature in build-progress.json does NOT reset .build_loop.feature in process-state.json. This is a stale-loop carryover that lets the immediate next feat: commit slip through. Recorded as a Phase 2 framework bug below.

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_blocks_commit
tests.details: Wrote sentinel via `scripts/pending-approval.sh --offer 'Approve?' --options 'A1: yes' 'A2: no' --recommendation 'A1'`. Hook denied a chore commit with the rich `Pending question:` message and full options/recommendation/offered_at echoed back.

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_resolves
tests.details: scripts/pending-approval.sh --resolve removed the sentinel; subsequent simulated chore commit returned EXIT=0 from the gate (no JSON deny output).

tests.name: recorded_feature_appears_in_progress
tests.details: test-gate.sh --record-feature uat-feat-1 succeeded; .claude/build-progress.json `.features_completed` = ['uat-feat-1']. Side-effect: emitted `[: null: integer expression expected` to stderr (recorded as Phase 0 medium bug).

framework_bugs.summary: Completing build_loop:feature_recorded does not reset .build_loop in process-state.json — next feat: commit bypasses BL-006
framework_bugs.reproduction: Walk a feature through all 6 build_loop steps (including feature_recorded). Inspect .claude/process-state.json: .build_loop.feature is still set to the just-completed feature name and .build_loop.steps_completed still has all 6 entries. Now attempt a new `git commit -m 'feat(other): something'`. The pre-commit-gate.sh sees .build_loop.feature is non-null AND all 5 required steps are completed (because they belong to the previous feature), so it allows the new feat: commit without requiring a fresh Build Loop.
framework_bugs.expected: Completing build_loop:feature_recorded should atomically reset .build_loop = {feature: null, step: 0, steps_completed: [], started_at: null} so the next feat: commit triggers a fresh BL-006 'no Build Loop active' check unless a new --start-feature is run.
framework_bugs.actual: feature_recorded marks the step in build-progress.json but leaves the build_loop block in process-state.json untouched. Stale loop satisfies require_build_loop_state_for_commit() forever until .build_loop is manually cleared or another --start-feature is run.

documentation_gaps.gap: The pending-approval sentinel CLI is `--offer QUESTION --options 'A1: ...' 'A2: ...' --recommendation 'A1'` (positional repeats for --options, NOT semicolon-separated). Runbook hint 'options A) yes;B) no' format is misleading; the script rejects single-string options.
documentation_gaps.where: Reports/uat-2026-04-25/RUNBOOK.md Section C bl015_sentinel_blocks_commit row should show the canonical CLI with --options used multiple times.

documentation_gaps.gap: The split-enforcement story — `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (when installed by init.sh) for secrets/SAST/TDD vs `scripts/pre-commit-gate.sh` (PreToolUse) for BL-006 / BL-015 / phase gates — is not documented in builders-guide.md.

tests.name: each_uat_step_completes

tests.name: phase3_to_phase4_gate

Update .claude/phase-state.json and APPROVAL_LOG.md to match.` Confirms agent-1's finding that the gate cross-checks APPROVAL_LOG.md entries that the framework provides no script to write. Bumped current_phase manually.

documentation_gaps.gap: phase3_validation steps (integration_testing, security_hardening, chaos_testing, accessibility_audit, performance_audit, contract_testing, results_archived, pre_launch_preparation, legal_review) are defined only in process-checklist.sh:31 — undocumented in builders-guide.md.

tests.name: start_phase4_exits_zero
tests.details: process-checklist.sh --start-phase4 exited 0.

tests.name: each_phase4_step_completes
tests.details: All 6 phase4_release steps eventually completed. PHASE 4 ORDERING ISSUE: monitoring_configured (step 4) requires HANDOFF.md to exist with monitoring keywords, but handoff_written is step 5. Worked around by writing HANDOFF.md with monitoring section before completing step 4 — recorded as a High framework bug.

tests.name: handoff_md_required
tests.details: handoff_written gate detects missing HANDOFF.md (returns 'Artifact check failed') and passes once HANDOFF.md exists. Confirmed.

tests.name: release_notes_required
tests.details: go_live_verified gate detects missing RELEASE_NOTES.md (already-present in skeleton from templates/generated/release-notes.tmpl).

tests.name: phase4_completion_state
tests.details: .claude/phase-state.json .current_phase = 4 (manually advanced via jq before --start-phase4 because no script does this). All 6 phase4_release steps_completed.

documentation_gaps.gap: PHASE4_STEPS step ordering (production_build rollback_tested go_live_verified monitoring_configured handoff_written handoff_tested) contradicts the monitoring_configured artifact gate that requires HANDOFF.md (which is created by handoff_written). Builders guide should clarify which is canonical and the framework should fix the other.

tests.name: no_uncommitted_secrets
tests.details: git ls-files | xargs grep for actual secret-pattern values (16+ alphanumeric chars after =) yielded zero hits. Keyword matches (password, token, secret) are all legitimate references in framework code/docs (e.g. GITHUB_TOKEN env var, scripts that talk about token detection).

tests.name: log_files_consistent
tests.details: N/A — .claude/process-audit.log is absent; no FORCE/reset events occurred during this run.

tests.name: no_orphaned_tempfiles
tests.details: find .claude -name '*.tmp' returned no results.

summary: init.sh has a Critical CPU-burning hang on stdin EOF (prompt_choice infinite loop) plus the High issues already known from agent-1 (no scriptable mode, no phase-advance script). New finding: PHASE4 step ordering is internally contradictory (monitoring_configured wants HANDOFF.md before handoff_written exists). New finding: completing build_loop:feature_recorded leaves .build_loop populated, allowing the next feat: commit to bypass BL-006. Sections B/C/D/E completed end-to-end via the runbook's documented manual-skeleton fallback path.
